title: Version specific command line reference
body:
/kind feature

**Description**
The Podman command reference only shows the latest cli, and includes commands that weren't available in the previous version. Sometimes companies need to stick on a version for a while (a few months, or even a year) and there should be a way to view matching documentation.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Go to podman.io, Documentation > Commands.

**Describe the results you received:**
The documentation lists a `podman machine inspect` that didn't exist in 4.0x (which is what I'm assuming I still have; version command is broken).

**Describe the results you expected:**
Defaulting to the current/latest release is fine, but there should be a way to view commands from previous major versions, especially when previous = "last month".

If that's not possible, assuming the command line is always backward compatible it would be enough to include tags like "Added in 4.1" to anything new.

comments.body: https://docs.podman.io allows you to change the version in the lower right corner.
🤦🏼‍♂️ I'll admit I'm an idiot, but any chance we could spell out 'version' or make it more prominent? I even looked for an option like this but it was not prominent/obvious enough.
This is the default readthedocs behaviour AFAIK. I have no idea if this can be changed but I agree that it is not very obvious.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since the docs.podman.io allows you to change the version I don't see us changing it, unless someone comes in with a way to make it more prominant.
